title:	Antigravity Arch Installer + Advanced Tool Use Infrastructure
state:	MERGED
author:	ehanc69
labels:	
assignees:

projects:	
milestone:	
number:	287
url:	https://github.com/ehanc69/aiyou-fastapi-services/pull/287
additions:	1982
deletions:	524
auto-merge:	disabled
--
## Summary
- **Antigravity Arch Installer**: Production-grade installer for Google Antigravity on Arch Linux
  - AVX2 pre-flight check (prevents silent crashes on incompatible CPUs)
  - SUID sandbox fix (`chmod 4755 chrome-sandbox`)
  - inotify tuning (524288 watches for agentic workloads)
  - Desktop entry with wrapper script

- **Advanced Tool Use patterns** (per Claude Developer Platform announcement):
  - Tool Search: 85% token savings via dynamic loading
  - Programmatic Tool Calling: 37% fewer round-trips
  - Implementation docs in `.claude/docs/`

## Files Changed (18)
- `scripts/antigravity_arch_installer.sh` - Main installer
- `src/aiyou/agents/base.py` - Agent base class
- `src/aiyou/tools/registry.py` - Tool registry
- `n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss_v8_full.py` - Complete swarm implementation

## Test plan
- [x] Installer script syntax check
- [ ] Run on Arch Linux VM
- [ ] Verify SUID sandbox permissions

content.content:
title:	Async Teleport + Training Data Safety Indexer
state:	MERGED
author:	ehanc69
labels:	
assignees:	
reviewers:	gemini-code-assist (Commented)
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	288
url:	https://github.com/ehanc69/aiyou-fastapi-services/pull/288
additions:	1180
deletions:	0
auto-merge:	disabled
--
## Summary
- Add Claude Code async teleport wrapper for non-blocking long-running tasks
- Implement training data safety indexer based on Apertus research (arxiv:2510.09471)
- Integrate p99 kill-switch for governance enforcement

## New Files

### Async Claude Teleport
- `scripts/async/claude_async.sh` - Non-blocking task wrapper with:
  - Teleport URL capture for conversation continuity across devices
  - Local logging + manifest (`/var/log/pnkln/claude-async/`)
  - p99 kill-switch with strict trigger conditions
  - Guardrails: no secrets, no migrations, no prod data

### Training Data Safety
- `pnkln/safety/training_data_indexer.py` - Full-text indexing for safety auditing:
  - Elasticsearch backend (Arm64/GCP compatible)
  - Multilingual lexicons (Weaponized Words, LDNOOBW, chemical weapons)
  - SafetyGate pre-governance content filtering
  - Performance: ~10k docs/sec English, ~600 docs/sec multilingual

### SOP Documentation
- `docs/sop/SOP-Async-Claude.md` - Usage policy with:
  - Allowed/forbidden use cases (ShadowTag analysis, JR Engine analytics)
  - Kill-switch protocol and triggers
  - 7-day trial metrics (time savings, failure rates, token reduction)

## Integration Points
- SafetyGate blocks critical content (CSAM, chemical weapons) before Judge #6
- Async wrapper enables parallel ShadowTag/JR Engine analysis
- p99 governance via kill-switch enforcement

## Revenue Opportunities
- ShadowTag Monitored Tier: $0.01/min with live job status
- Kernel Chain Async Batch: bulk decision processing
- Judge #6 Governance Replay: $500+/month for regulated industries

## Test plan
- [ ] Run `./scripts/async/claude_async.sh help` to verify script
- [ ] Test safety indexer: `python3 -m pnkln.safety.training_data_indexer scan -f test.txt`
- [ ] Verify kill-switch activation/deactivation
- [ ] 7-day trial with non-production workloads

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

content.content:
title:	UnGPT v2.0: Static vs Dynamic Multi-Model Pipeline
state:	MERGED
author:	ehanc69
labels:	
assignees:	
reviewers:	gemini-code-assist (Commented)
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	289
url:	https://github.com/ehanc69/aiyou-fastapi-services/pull/289
additions:	3631
deletions:	0
auto-merge:	disabled
--
## Summary
- **7-layer multi-model pipeline** with Static vs Dynamic architecture
- **Static Truth (SuperGrok)**: L0, L3, L5, L7 - Intent validation, sanity checks
- **Dynamic Execution (Claude)**: L1, L4, L6 - Code execution, publishing
- **Infinite Loop (Gemini↔GPT)**: L2 - Generative horsepower with convergence detection

## Architecture
```
Voice → L0(Grok) → L1(Claude) → L2(Gemini↔GPT) → L3(Grok) → L4(Claude) → GitHub
                                                                    ↓
                                              L5(Grok) → L7(Voice) → Speaker
```

## Key Features
- **[REQ_EXECUTION] flagging**: L3 auto-tags code blocks that need execution
- **CRM scoring**: Score ≤3 aborts, 4-5 triggers re-loop with enhanced tools
- **SPT.1/SPT.2 validation**: Baseline expectations validated at each layer
- **Glass Box Forensic Index**: Elasticsearch-based compliance search with slop
- **PII scrubbing**: Automatic before forensic indexing

## Files Added (23 total)
- `ungpt/__init__.py`, `orchestrator.py`, `config.yaml`
- `ungpt/ungpt_v2_final.ipynb` - Complete notebook
- Agents: `supergrok_intake.py`, `claude_frame.py`, `gemini_refine.py`, `gpt5_refine.py`, `supergrok_validate.py`, `claude_execute.py`, `supergrok_repackage.py`, `claude_pr_check.py`, `supergrok_voice.py`
- Utils: `retry_logic.py`, `crm_analytics.py`, `github_push.py`
- Forensic: `glass_box_indexer.py`, `es_config.py`, `pii_scrubber.py`

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify orchestrator imports cleanly
- [ ] Test forensic indexer in mock mode
- [ ] Run notebook cells 1-4
- [ ] Validate PII scrubber patterns

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

content.content:
title:	Pingora Media Edge: Rust HLS Proxy with Cloud CDN
state:	MERGED
author:	ehanc69
labels:	
assignees:	
reviewers:	chatgpt-codex-connector (Commented), gemini-code-assist (Commented)
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	290
url:	https://github.com/ehanc69/aiyou-fastapi-services/pull/290
additions:	1904
deletions:	0
auto-merge:	disabled
--
## Summary
- **New Rust service** for high-performance HLS video streaming using Pingora v0.4 (Cloudflare's production proxy framework)
- **TinyUfo cache** - O(1) algorithm with 512MB MVP capacity (superior to LRU for streaming workloads)
- **Cloud CDN enabled** - GCP edge caching via BackendConfig (1h default TTL, 24h max)
- **JWT authentication** matching Python backend (HS256)
- **GKE Autopilot deployment** with HPA (3-20 replicas), Spot VMs, and distroless container

## Architecture
```
┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐
│  Cloud CDN   │ → │ media-edge   │ → │  TinyUfo     │ → │    GCS       │
│  (L7 LB)     │    │  (Pingora)   │    │   Cache      │    │   Bucket     │
└──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘
```

## Key Files
| Path | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `media-edge/src/proxy.rs` | Core Pingora ProxyHttp implementation |
| `media-edge/src/cache.rs` | TinyUfo cache wrapper with TTL tracking |
| `media-edge/src/gcs.rs` | GCS backend with ADC authentication |
| `media-edge/src/auth.rs` | JWT verification (HS256) |
| `media-edge/k8s/` | K8s manifests (Deployment, HPA, Service, BackendConfig) |
| `cloudbuild.media-edge.yaml` | CI/CD pipeline with vulnerability scanning |

## Design Decisions
- **Panic-free code**: `Result`/`Option` instead of `unwrap()` (learned from Nov 2025 outage research)
- **Multi-stage Docker**: cargo-chef pattern for faster builds
- **Cache-Control headers**: Proper CDN integration with `X-Cache: HIT/MISS`
- **Graceful shutdown**: 60s connection draining

## Test plan
- [ ] `cargo test --release` passes in Cloud Build
- [ ] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean
- [ ] Verify health endpoints (`/health`, `/ready`) respond correctly
- [ ] Test HLS segment serving with mock GCS
- [ ] Validate Cloud CDN cache headers in staging

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

content.text:
Perfect! I now have all the PR details. Let me create a structured summary table analyzing these 5 merged PRs.

## PR Analysis Summary

| # | PR Title | What Was Added | Key Files Changed | Business Value |
|---|----------|-----------------|-------------------|-----------------|
| **286** | n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss v8: Multi-Model Routing | MultiModelRouter orchestration routing requests to Gemini (reads) and Claude (reasoning) based on workload type; Enterprise Compliance API + SDK | Core routing logic in multi-model orchestration layer; API + SDK implementations | **83% cost savings** on bulk document analysis: $4.50 → $0.77 per 50 files; enables enterprise compliance at scale |
| **287** | Antigravity Arch Installer + Advanced Tool Use Infrastructure | Production installer for Google Antigravity on Arch Linux with AVX2 pre-flight checks, SUID sandbox fixes, inotify tuning; Advanced Tool Use patterns (Tool Search, Programmatic Tool Calling) | `scripts/antigravity_arch_installer.sh`, `src/aiyou/agents/base.py`, `src/aiyou/tools/registry.py`, `n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss_v8_full.py` (18 files) | **37% fewer round-trips** via programmatic tool calling; **85% token savings** via dynamic tool loading; prevents silent crashes on incompatible CPUs |
| **288** | Async Teleport + Training Data Safety Indexer | Async wrapper for non-blocking long-running tasks with teleport URL capture; Training data safety indexer using Apertus research (arxiv:2510.09471); p99 kill-switch for governance | `scripts/async/claude_async.sh`, `pnkln/safety/training_data_indexer.py`, `docs/sop/SOP-Async-Claude.md` (new files) | **10k docs/sec** indexing (English), **600 docs/sec** (multilingual); enables parallel ShadowTag/JR Engine analysis; **ShadowTag Monitored Tier** revenue: $0.01/min; **Judge #6 Governance**: $500+/month |
| **289** | UnGPT v2.0: Static vs Dynamic Multi-Model Pipeline | 7-layer multi-model pipeline with static truth (SuperGrok at L0, L3, L5, L7) and dynamic execution (Claude at L1, L4, L6); infinite loop convergence (Gemini↔GPT); Glass Box Forensic Index with Elasticsearch; PII scrubbing | 23 new files: `ungpt/orchestrator.py`, agents (`supergrok_intake.py`, `claude_frame.py`, `gemini_refine.py`), forensic tools (`glass_box_indexer.py`, `pii_scrubber.py`), utilities | **Compliance audit trail** via Glass Box Forensic Index; **CRM scoring** prevents invalid executions (≤3 aborts); automated **PII scrubbing** before indexing; structured multi-layer validation |
| **290** | Pingora Media Edge: Rust HLS Proxy with Cloud CDN | High-performance HLS video streaming proxy in Rust using Pingora v0.4; TinyUfo O(1) cache algorithm (512MB MVP); Cloud CDN integration with GCP edge caching (1h/24h TTL); JWT auth; GKE Autopilot deployment | `media-edge/src/proxy.rs`, `media-edge/src/cache.rs`, `media-edge/src/gcs.rs`, `media-edge/src/auth.rs`, `media-edge/k8s/`, `cloudbuild.media-edge.yaml` | **Superior cache performance** vs LRU for streaming; **Cloud CDN edge caching** reduces origin load; **3-20 pod autoscaling** with Spot VMs reduces cost; **panic-free Rust** prevents runtime crashes; enables video delivery at scale |

## Key Insights

**Technology Evolution:**
- **Multi-model orchestration** (PR #286, #289) - Leveraging different AI models for cost/performance optimization
- **Safety & Governance** (PR #288, #289) - Advanced indexing and kill-switches for compliance
- **Infrastructure scaling** (PR #290) - Rust-based edge services replacing Python for video streaming

**Cost Impact:**
- PR #286: 83% cost reduction on document analysis
- PR #287: 85% token reduction via smart tool loading
- PR #290: Spot VM usage + efficient caching reduces operational costs

**Operational Value:**
- PR #288: 10k docs/sec processing capability opens SaaS revenue streams
- PR #289: Compliance-first architecture with forensic traceability
- PR #290: Production-grade video delivery with automated scaling
